title: Nonlinear dynamic elastic moduli from equilibrium stress fluctuations
authors: F. E. Garbuzov, Y. M. Beltukov
abstract: Fluctuation formulas for elastic and viscoelastic moduli allow their computation from equilibrium molecular dynamics simulations, avoiding explicit nonequilibrium deformation protocols. While such expressions are well established for the quasi-static moduli, and also the linear dynamic moduli, no fluctuation formula exists for the nonlinear time-dependent moduli that govern anharmonic viscoelastic response under finite time-dependent strains. In this work we derive transient-time correlation function expressions for both the linear and the nonlinear dynamic moduli, starting from the DOLLS/SLLOD equations of motion for irrotational motion. The resulting formulas involve equilibrium time correlations of the stress tensor and Born-kinetic terms, and they recover the known quasi-static and linear dynamic results in the appropriate limits.
summary: This paper provides new fluctuation formulas that allow researchers to calculate nonlinear dynamic elastic moduli using only equilibrium molecular dynamics data. This avoids the need for expensive nonequilibrium simulations of finite-strain deformation. The resulting framework unifies linear viscoelasticity and nonlinear dynamic response within a single statistical mechanics formalism.
main_problem: The lack of established fluctuation formulas to compute nonlinear time-dependent (dynamic) elastic moduli from equilibrium molecular dynamics simulations.
main_result: The derivation of transient-time correlation function expressions for both linear and nonlinear dynamic moduli, which recover known quasi-static and linear dynamic results in appropriate limits.
method: A theoretical derivation using the DOLLS/SLLOD equations of motion, a power series expansion of the nonequilibrium average stress as a functional of strain history, and a pulled-back variable transformation.
model_or_system: A classical system of N interacting particles with a Hamiltonian consisting of kinetic and potential energy, modeled using DOLLS/SLLOD dynamics for irrotational motion.
key_observables: Linear dynamic viscoelastic moduli tensor (C_dyn), nonlinear dynamic moduli tensor (N_dyn), Second Piola-Kirchhoff stress tensor (S), and Cauchy stress tensor (sigma).
important_parameters: Small but finite time-dependent strains (anharmonic regime), Born-kinetic terms, and the limit of zero-frequency (quasi-static) response.
main_assumptions: The motion is irrotational (L = L^T), the potential is translationally and rotationally invariant, the initial state is a canonical ensemble, and the system follows classical Cauchy elasticity.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the scientific problem and the microscopic equations of motion, proceeds to a mathematical derivation using pulled-back variables and power series expansions of stress, demonstrates the recovery of known limits, and concludes with discussions on symmetry and limitations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
